core_behavior: The system must automatically retry HTTP API requests that fail due to transient errors, without requiring intervention from the caller.
Retry attempts must follow a configurable exponential backoff strategy with jitter to avoid thundering-herd problems.
After exhausting all retry attempts, the system must surface the final failure to the caller with full diagnostic context.
The retry mechanism must be implemented as a reusable, composable layer (e.g., middleware, decorator, or interceptor) that wraps outbound API calls and is transparent to upstream business logic.
If any attempt succeeds (HTTP status code not in `retryable_status_codes` and no retryable exception), the system returns the HTTP response object immediately. No further attempts are made.
If all attempts are exhausted, the system raises or returns a structured `RetryExhaustedError`.
A failed attempt is eligible for retry if the failure is a retryable status code OR a retryable exception type.
inputs: `max_attempts` (integer): Total number of attempts including the initial request
`retryable_status_codes` (list<int>): HTTP status codes that should trigger a retry
`retryable_exceptions` (list<ExceptionType>): Exception/error types that should trigger a retry
`base_delay_ms` (integer): Base delay in milliseconds for backoff calculation
`max_delay_ms` (integer): Upper cap on delay between retries
`timeout_per_attempt_ms` (integer): Per-attempt timeout independent of total timeout
`total_timeout_ms` (integer): Hard deadline across all attempts combined
`jitter_strategy` (enum): `NONE`, `FULL`, `EQUAL`
`retry_on_timeout` (boolean): Whether a per-attempt timeout counts as retryable
Global default `max_attempts`: 3
Global default `retryable_status_codes`: 429, 500, 502, 503, 504
Global default `retryable_exceptions`: `ConnectionError`, `TimeoutError`, `NetworkError`
Global default `base_delay_ms`: 200
Global default `max_delay_ms`: 10,000
Global default `total_timeout_ms`: 30,000
Global default `jitter_strategy`: `FULL`
Global default `retry_on_timeout`: true
The system accepts any well-formed HTTP request (method, URL, headers, body, query parameters) as the unit of work to be retried.
The request object must be treated as immutable across attempts; the same request is replayed each time.
Per-request configuration overrides global defaults.
outputs: On success, the system returns the HTTP response object immediately.

On exhausted retries, the system raises or returns a structured `RetryExhaustedError` containing: `final_error` (the last exception or HTTP response received), `attempts_made` (integer count of total attempts executed), `total_elapsed_ms` (total wall-clock time across all attempts), `attempt_log` (ordered list of per-attempt records), and `request_id` (correlation ID for tracing).

Each entry in `attempt_log` contains: `attempt_number` (1-based index), `outcome` (`SUCCESS`, `RETRYABLE_FAILURE`, `NON_RETRYABLE_FAILURE`, `TIMEOUT`), `http_status_code` (integer or null if exception occurred before response), `exception_type` (string or null if HTTP response was received), `duration_ms` (time taken for that attempt), `delay_before_next_ms` (delay applied after this attempt, 0 for last attempt).
assumptions: The request object must be treated as immutable across attempts; the same request is replayed each time.
failure_modes: After exhausting all retry attempts, the system must surface the final failure to the caller with full diagnostic context.
If all attempts are exhausted, the system raises or returns a structured `RetryExhaustedError`.
edge_cases: `retry_on_timeout` (boolean): Whether a per-attempt timeout counts as retryable
`delay_before_next_ms`: delay applied after this attempt (0 for last attempt)
`http_status_code`: integer or null if exception occurred before response
`exception_type`: string or null if HTTP response was received
performance_constraints: Retry attempts must follow a configurable exponential backoff strategy with jitter to avoid thundering-herd problems.
`max_delay_ms` (integer): Upper cap on delay between retries
`timeout_per_attempt_ms` (integer): Per-attempt timeout independent of total timeout
`total_timeout_ms` (integer): Hard deadline across all attempts combined
